claim: Bristol Myers Squibb agreed to acquire Karuna Therapeutics for $14.0B ($330/share cash) to obtain KarXT (xanomeline-trospium), a first-in-class M1/M4 muscarinic agonist antipsychotic, with a schizophrenia PDUFA of 26 Sep 2024.
company: Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE: BMY) / Karuna Therapeutics (NASDAQ: KRTX)
figure: $14.0B equity value; $330.00/share cash; $12.7B net of cash
deal_structure: all-cash buyout of a pre-approval asset (no milestone/CVR) -> high implied PoS
implication: A $14B all-cash buyout of a not-yet-approved CNS asset signals high implied probability of success for the muscarinic mechanism; KarXT (later Cobenfy) won FDA approval Sep 2024, validating the bet.
source: SEC 8-K (Bristol Myers Squibb), Exhibit 99.1 joint press release
quote: Bristol Myers Squibb has agreed to acquire Karuna for $330.00 per share in cash, for a total equity value of $14.0 billion, or $12.7 billion net of estimated cash acquired.

claim: AbbVie agreed to acquire Cerevel Therapeutics for ~$8.7B ($45/share cash), centered on emraclidine, an M4 positive allosteric modulator positioned as a next-generation schizophrenia antipsychotic (plus tavapadon for Parkinson's).
deal_structure: all-cash buyout of clinical-stage pipeline -> high implied PoS on emraclidine
implication: AbbVie's $8.7B bet rode the same muscarinic thesis as the BMS/Karuna deal; the implied high PoS was later contradicted by emraclidine's Phase 2 failure (next card) - illustrating concentration risk in single-mechanism M&A.
quote: Transaction valued at $45.00 per share in cash, for a total equity value of approximately $8.7 billion

claim: Emraclidine, the centerpiece of AbbVie's $8.7B Cerevel acquisition, failed both Phase 2 EMPOWER-1 and EMPOWER-2 schizophrenia trials (Nov 2024), missing the PANSS primary endpoint vs placebo - triggering a large impairment of the deal's value.
figure: Phase 2 EMPOWER-1/-2 primary-endpoint miss; multibillion-dollar Cerevel impairment (reported ~$3.5B; AbbVie SEC filing not fetched this pass)
implication: Realized risk: the muscarinic thesis that paid off for BMS (KarXT) did not generalize to AbbVie's M4 PAM. Deal value concentrated in one mechanism is fragile - a single Phase 2 readout erased much of the $8.7B premise.
quote: did not meet their primary endpoint of showing a statistically significant reduction (improvement) in the change from baseline in the Positive and Negative Syndrome Scale (PANSS) total score compared to the placebo group at week 6.

claim: Johnson & Johnson agreed to acquire Intra-Cellular Therapies for ~$14.6B ($132/share cash), gaining CAPLYTA (lumateperone) - an approved CNS franchise for bipolar depression and schizophrenia, with a pending MDD label-expansion sNDA.
company: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) / Intra-Cellular Therapies (NASDAQ: ITCI)
figure: ~$14.6B equity value; $132.00/share cash; CAPLYTA $5B+ peak-sales potential
deal_structure: all-cash buyout of an on-market, revenue-generating franchise -> lower-risk, commercial-stage bet
implication: Continued big-pharma consolidation into CNS at scale; unlike the pre-approval Karuna/Cerevel bets, J&J paid up for an *approved* asset with label-expansion upside - a lower-PoS-risk profile reflected in the premium for de-risked revenue.
quote: Johnson & Johnson will acquire all outstanding shares of Intra-Cellular Therapies, a biopharmaceutical company focused on the development and commercialization of therapeutics for central nervous system (CNS) disorders, for $132.00 per share in cash for a total equity value of approximately $14.6 billion.

claim: Neumora's navacaprant (kappa-opioid receptor antagonist) failed the Phase 3 KOASTAL-1 MDD trial (2 Jan 2025), missing the MADRS primary endpoint vs placebo; shares fell sharply (~80%, per press) and the full KOASTAL program was later discontinued with a workforce cut.
figure: KOASTAL-1 primary-endpoint miss; stock ~-80% (press); ~35% workforce reduction (later)
implication: CNS/MDD remains high-risk: a novel-mechanism antidepressant can erase most of a company's market value in a single readout. The deal/financing implication - thin runway, restructuring - follows directly from the clinical miss.
quote: Navacaprant did not achieve primary or secondary endpoints in the KOASTAL-1 phase 3 trial for MDD. Both navacaprant and placebo showed similar improvements in depression scores, with no significant difference.
primary_source_note: Canonical primary is Neumora's IR press release (ir.neumoratx.com, 2 Jan 2025), which is browser-loadable but curl-unreachable from this environment; cited via Psychiatric Times (faithful secondary, curl-verifiable).

claim: KarXT (xanomeline-trospium) showed a significant antipsychotic effect in the Phase 2 EMERGENT-1 schizophrenia trial - PANSS total fell 11.6 points more than placebo (p<0.001), without D2 blockade - the muscarinic-agonist proof-of-concept underpinning BMS's $14B Karuna acquisition.
class_basis: M1/M4 muscarinic agonism reduces psychosis without dopamine D2 blockade (EMERGENT-1 Phase 2, NCT03697252, n=182)
implication: Grounds the implied PoS in the BMS/Karuna ($14B) and AbbVie/Cerevel ($8.7B) muscarinic bets; the mechanism is clinically validated for KarXT even though AbbVie's M4-PAM emraclidine later failed Phase 2.
quote: The change from baseline to week 5 was -17.4 points with xanomeline-trospium and -5.9 points with placebo (least-squares mean difference, -11.6 points; 95% confidence interval, -16.1 to -7.1; P<0.001).
